name: Tallinn (Estonia)
description: Six equal horizontal stripes alternating blue and white (blue at the top and bottom, with three blue stripes total separated by two white stripes), in the city's traditional civic colours.
history: Tallinn's white-and-blue civic colours derive from the city's medieval coat of arms. The horizontally-banded flag in this form is the modern municipal flag adopted by the Tallinn City Council.
